summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that user-controlled filenames flow into filepath.Join() without sanitization, allowing path traversal via ../ sequences and absolute paths to write files to arbitrary locations on Proxmox nodes.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that filenames containing ../ sequences or absolute paths escape the intended upload directory when processed by the vulnerable filepath.Join code in NodeUpload and NodeStreamUpload.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the container, mounted the target repository, and verified that multiple path traversal vectors (../../../etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, ../../root/.ssh/authorized_keys, ../../../../etc/cron.d/payload) escape the base directory. The source_raw.file_name attribute was confirmed to have no validation.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks the target repo for the vulnerable filepath.Join pattern, tests multiple traversal payloads via Python os.path.join (matching Go filepath.Join behavior), and confirms that file_name attributes lack validation. Success requires at least 3 traversal vectors confirmed.
evidence_produced_by_run: The vulnerable filepath.Join(remoteFileDir, d.FileName) pattern found at proxmox/ssh/client.go
source_raw.file_name attribute confirmed to have no ValidateDiagFunc
Proof file /tmp/EVOHUNT_PATH_TRAVERSAL_PROOF created upon successful verification

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
